message.content:
Here's the prompt for your backend team:

  ---
  Backend task: Default currency to AED for all users

  Context:
  The frontend reads user settings via GET /profile/settings (returns { CURRENCY: SupportedCurrency, NOTIFICATIONS: ... }). The
  currency sync provider now falls back to AED if the backend returns no CURRENCY value, but the ideal fix is that the backend always
  returns a value.

  Required changes:

  1. User registration / account creation: When a new user is created, save CURRENCY = "AED" as their default setting in the user
  settings store.
  2. Settings GET endpoint: When returning user settings and CURRENCY is not set (null/undefined), return "AED" as the default value
  instead of omitting the field.
  3. Migration for existing users: Run a one-time migration to set CURRENCY = "AED" for all users who currently have no currency
  setting.

  Expected result: GET /profile/settings always returns { "CURRENCY": "AED", ... } for any user who hasn't explicitly chosen a
  different currency.

  ---
  The frontend change I made already handles the fallback: once settings load, currency is always set to AED if the backend returns
  nothing — overriding any stale localStorage value.

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. There's an existing migration that sets USD as default, but the requirement is AED. I need to update 4 places: the default currency constant, the entity default, the migration, and add a fallback in `getSettings`.

content.text: Now update `getSettings` in the user service to return AED as fallback for users missing the setting:

content.content: The file /Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-be/apps/api/src/modules/user/services/user.service.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
All 4 changes are done:

1. **`src/common/currency.ts`** — `DEFAULT_CURRENCY` changed from `'USD'` to `'AED'`
2. **`src/db/entities/user.entity.ts`** — Column default changed from `'USD'` to `'AED'` (applies to new users created via TypeORM)
3. **`src/db/migrations/1774224000000-user-currency-setting.ts`** — Migration updated to backfill `AED` for existing users without a currency setting, and sets the column-level DB default to `AED`
4. **`src/modules/user/services/user.service.ts`** — `getSettings` now falls back to `DEFAULT_CURRENCY` (`'AED'`) if `CURRENCY` is missing, so `GET /profile/settings` always returns a value

Run `pnpm db:run` to apply the migration to existing users.

addedBlocks:
## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:figma:figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]

Готово. Старая миграция восстановлена как была. Изменения:

- **`1774224000000-user-currency-setting.ts`** — откатил обратно к USD
- **`1775563283158-default-currency-aed.ts`** — новая миграция: обновляет всех существующих пользователей с USD → AED (или без currency), и меняет column default на AED

`down()` меняет AED → USD, чтобы откат был безопасным.
